top_signals.title: Money Funds Keep Cash Closer as Fed Leaves Markets Guessing
top_signals.summary: Money‑market funds are shifting toward ultra short-term holdings and away from assets with even modest interest-rate risk as uncertainty grows over the Federal Reserve’s policy path and the near-term outlook for rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gilts Rattled by New UK PM’s Comment on Fiscal Flexibility
top_signals.summary: Gilts extended their retreat and the pound slipped after UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham said he will seek “any flexibility” in the government’s fiscal rules, stoking concerns his administration will add to the nation’s already-heavy debt load.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: PCT: Double Recognition for Responsible Growth and Sustainable Development
top_signals.summary: PCT, a 100% subsidiary of COSCO SHIPPING Ports Ltd., has been honoured with the Social and Economic Impact Excellence Award as part of the prestigious “Protagonists of the Greek Economy” awards, organized by Direction. top_signals.title: No CDL, DUI and out-of-service orders: Commercial vehicle enforcement ramps up
top_signals.summary:
Law enforcement agencies across the U.S. are stepping up commercial vehicle enforcement, with recent cases ranging from unlicensed hotshot drivers to impaired truckers and dangerous equipment violations.

top_signals.title: Nigeria Targets Record 3 Million Bpd Oil Output by 2030
top_signals.summary: Nigeria is set to ramp up crude oil production to 3 million barrels per day by 2030, according to the country’s upstream regulator, which says reforms aimed at accelerating investment and reducing project delays are beginning to reverse years of declining output. The target would nearly double the country’s current crude production and mark the highest level in Nigeria’s history, according to Bloomberg.  The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) said the government’s objective remains achievable…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Amphitrite reinforces war offering with new underwriting hire
top_signals.summary: Amphitrite Underwriting London (“Amphitrite”), the specialist marine MGA, today announces the appointment of Jack Thompson as war underwriter, effective immediately. Based in London, Thompson will report to Ben Jones and will support the further development of Amphitrite’s marine war offering, delivering responsive and efficient solutions to support the evolving needs of the marine industry. Thompson ...

top_signals.title: HD Hyundai Heavy Industries halts production after fatal Ulsan shipyard accident
top_signals.summary: HD Hyundai Heavy Industries will halt production at all plants for a day on the 21st. The move follows a serious industrial accident in which a field worker at the Ulsan shipyard died. HD Hyundai Heavy Industries said on the 20th that it will “halt production at all plants on the 21st to conduct special ...

top_signals.title: Kevin Price starts as President of Georgia Ports
top_signals.summary: Kevin Price has officially stepped into his position as President of the Georgia Ports Authority on July 1, 2026. “I’m excited for our employees and all our stakeholders because Kevin is the perfect candidate to take GPA and our Team to the next level,” said CEO Griff Lynch. GPA announced Price as the new President ...

top_signals.title: Wärtsilä and Carnival Corporation extend long-standing collaboration with Lifecycle Agreement supporting LNG-powered cruise fleet performance
top_signals.summary: Technology group Wärtsilä and Carnival Corporation (NYSE: CCL), the world’s largest cruise company, have entered into an 8-year Lifecycle Agreement covering four cruise vessels within the Princess Cruises and Carnival Cruise Line fleets, including two newbuild vessels currently under construction. The agreement marks the first service contract between the companies covering Carnival Corporation’s LNG-fuelled cruise ...
